entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: An important food crop for people in semi-arid Africa.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Region where sorghum is an important food crop.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to withstand water stress, specifically post-flowering drought.

entities.name: POST-FLOWERING DROUGHT
entities.description: Drought occurring after the flowering stage that affects sorghum.

entities.name: BC2F4 SORGHUM GENOTYPES
entities.description: Specific sorghum genotypes used to screen traits contributing to drought tolerance.

entities.name: SPLIT-PLOT DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to evaluate sorghum genotypes under different water conditions.

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: A condition where 0 mm/day of water is applied from post-flowering to plant maturity.

entities.name: WELL-WATERED CONDITION
entities.description: A condition where 7 mm/day of water is applied.

entities.name: SE438
entities.description: A sorghum genotype that produced the highest grain yield in water-stressed environment.

entities.name: NA316C
entities.description: A sorghum genotype that yielded highest under well-watered environment.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced, used as a measure of performance and yield potential.

entities.name: GEOMETRIC MEAN PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: An index highly correlated with yield under well-watered and water-stressed conditions.

entities.name: MEAN PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: An index highly correlated with yield under well-watered and water-stressed conditions.

entities.name: FIRST PRINCIPAL AXIS
entities.description: A statistical indicator explaining 59.1% of total variation and the best indicator of yield potential and drought tolerance.
